etymology.description: 炒(炒) (chǎo, "stir-fry") refers to a method of cooking that involves frying food quickly in hot oil while stirring. 蛋(蛋) (dàn, "egg") is the common word for eggs. Together, 炒蛋 indicates a dish made by stir-frying eggs, resulting in scrambled eggs.
etymology.interpretation: 炒(炒) (chǎo, "stir-fry") involves quickly cooking food in hot oil with continuous stirring, which changes the texture of the egg proteins and creates a fluffy consistency. 蛋(蛋) (dàn, "egg") is the base ingredient. The combination evokes the process of agitating eggs over high heat to produce the dish known as scrambled eggs.

examples.chinese: 早餐我常常吃炒蛋(早餐我常常吃炒蛋)。
examples.english: I often eat scrambled eggs for breakfast.

examples.chinese: 炒蛋是快手的美味早餐(炒蛋是快手的美味早餐)。
examples.pinyin: Chǎodàn shì kuàishǒu de měiwèi zǎocān.
examples.english: Scrambled eggs are a quick and delicious breakfast.

examples.chinese: 为了增加味道(為了增加味道)，她在炒蛋里加了香葱(她在炒蛋裡加了香蔥)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile zēngjiā wèidào, tā zài chǎodàn lǐ jiāle xiāngcōng.
examples.english: To enhance the flavor, she added green onions to the scrambled eggs.
